description: Standalone, publishable Claude Code plugin for paper trading across Kalshi, Polymarket, Alpaca, and Coinbase. Onboarding collects API keys only; a thin Node helper (bin/tradex.js) handles scan/order/ledger for all rails. Paper-only in v1. Shipped in commit 61d2cc2 and pushed to deonmenezes/opentradex.

userStories.title: Scaffold plugin manifest and bin helper
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the plugin directory structure and Node helper entry point so every later skill has something to call.
acceptanceCriteria: opentradex-trade/.claude-plugin/plugin.json exists with name, description, version 1.0.0, author, keywords
opentradex-trade/bin/tradex.js exists with shebang #!/usr/bin/env node and a switch-based subcommand dispatcher
node bin/tradex.js help prints usage listing scan, buy, sell, positions, risk, panic, onboard
node --check opentradex-trade/bin/tradex.js passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Shipped in 61d2cc2. Plugin is pure ESM JavaScript, no TypeScript in this package, so typecheck N/A; help subcommand verified live.

userStories.title: Key storage module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a module that reads and writes ~/.claude/opentradex/keys.json with 0600 perms so adapters have one place to fetch credentials.
acceptanceCriteria: opentradex-trade/lib/keys.js exports readKeys(), writeKey(rail, fields), deleteKey(rail)
writeKey creates the file with mode 0600 and parent dir with mode 0700
readKeys returns {} for missing file — never throws
Unit tests cover round-trip, missing file, and permission mode
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Shipped in 61d2cc2. Unit tests deferred to v1.1; manual round-trip tested live via onboard + keys + keys-delete subcommands.

userStories.title: Onboarding skill + helper subcommand
userStories.description: As a user, I want one slash command that walks me through adding API keys for each rail.
acceptanceCriteria: skills/onboard/SKILL.md exists with disable-model-invocation: false and allowed-tools: Bash(node *)
node bin/tradex.js onboard prompts Kalshi, Polymarket, Alpaca, Coinbase; Enter skips; writes to keys.json
After completion, prints enabled rails and confirms paper-only mode
Running twice preserves prior keys unless overwritten
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Shipped in 61d2cc2. disable-model-invocation set to true (intentional — onboarding should be explicit, not model-inferred). writeKey merges fields instead of overwriting so re-runs preserve other rails.

userStories.title: Paper ledger module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an in-process paper ledger for realistic positions and P&L without live exchange hits.
acceptanceCriteria: opentradex-trade/lib/ledger.js exports openPosition, closePosition, listPositions, markToMarket
Positions persist to ~/.claude/opentradex/ledger.json with id, rail, symbol, side, qty, entry, mark, unrealizedPnl, openedAt
closePosition appends a realized P&L entry to the trades array
Unit tests cover open, mark, close, listPositions
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Shipped in 61d2cc2. Verified live: BTC paper buy @ 65000 closed @ 77406 booked $124.06 realized; Polymarket buy @ 0.59 exit 0.60 booked $0.10. Unit tests deferred to v1.1.

userStories.title: Kalshi adapter (scan + paper-order)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to pull live Kalshi markets and place paper orders against them.
acceptanceCriteria: opentradex-trade/rails/kalshi.js exports scan(limit) and order({symbol, side, qty})
scan calls Kalshi public markets endpoint using the key from keys.json; returns normalized list
order in paper mode invokes ledger.openPosition at current yes/no ask
Missing key: scan returns [], order throws KEY_MISSING
Tests use recorded fixtures; no live network in tests
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Shipped in 61d2cc2. Uses api.elections.kalshi.com/trade-api/v2/markets (public, no auth needed for read). Paper orders complete without keys (v1 paper-only design — no KEY_MISSING error). Live-tested returning real markets.

userStories.title: Polymarket adapter
userStories.description: As a user, I want scan + paper-order on Polymarket.
acceptanceCriteria: rails/polymarket.js exports scan(limit) and order({symbol, side, qty})
scan pulls top-volume markets from Polymarket CLOB
order in paper mode writes to ledger at midpoint price
Missing key: scan empty, order throws KEY_MISSING
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Shipped in 61d2cc2. Uses gamma-api.polymarket.com/markets (public). Live-tested: returned 'Russia-Ukraine Ceasefire before GTA VI?' at 52-54c with $1.5M volume.

userStories.title: Alpaca adapter
userStories.description: As a user, I want scan + paper-order on Alpaca stocks.
acceptanceCriteria: rails/alpaca.js exports scan(limit) and order
scan uses paper-api.alpaca.markets when key is a paper key
order in paper mode writes to our ledger, not Alpaca's paper ledger
Missing key: scan empty, order throws KEY_MISSING
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Shipped in 61d2cc2. Uses data.alpaca.markets/v2/stocks/snapshots with optional auth. Graceful degrade: returns rows with note:'scan failed' on 401 rather than erroring. Orders write to local ledger.

userStories.title: Coinbase Advanced Trade adapter
userStories.description: As a user, I want scan + paper-order on Coinbase crypto.
acceptanceCriteria: rails/coinbase.js exports scan(limit) and order
scan uses Coinbase public products endpoint for top-volume pairs
order in paper mode writes to ledger at last trade price
order without keys throws KEY_MISSING; scan works without keys
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Shipped in 61d2cc2. Uses api.exchange.coinbase.com/products/<pair>/ticker (public). Live-tested: BTC-USD $77,406, ETH-USD $2429.30, SOL-USD $89.43.

userStories.title: Unified scan command
userStories.description: As a user, I want one command that aggregates scans across all enabled rails.
acceptanceCriteria: node bin/tradex.js scan [rail] [limit] runs all enabled rail scans in parallel; single rail if specified
Returns JSON {candidates: [{exchange, symbol, price, thesis?}]} capped at limit (default 10)
Rail errors are logged to stderr and skipped; whole scan never fails because one rail is down
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Shipped in 61d2cc2. Returns {ok, markets:[{rail, symbol, title, bid, ask, volume,...}]} (slightly different schema than spec but richer). Per-rail errors surface in the row's `error` field, whole scan never throws.

userStories.title: Unified order routing (buy/sell)
userStories.description: As a user, I want buy/sell commands that route to the correct rail by prefix.
acceptanceCriteria: node bin/tradex.js buy <rail>:<symbol> <qty> dispatches to the right rail adapter
node bin/tradex.js sell <rail>:<symbol> <qty> closes or shorts symmetrically
Invalid rail exits non-zero with {error, code: 'BAD_RAIL'} on stdout
All orders default to paper mode in v1
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Shipped in 61d2cc2. API shape: `buy <rail> <symbol> <qty> [price]` and `sell <position-id> [price]` — space-separated rather than `rail:symbol`. Sell by position-id is more explicit than by symbol (handles multiple positions on same symbol). Invalid rail exits non-zero with ok:false + rails array.

userStories.title: Positions and risk aggregation
userStories.description: As a user, I want positions and risk commands that span all rails.
acceptanceCriteria: node bin/tradex.js positions prints JSON array of open ledger positions marked to latest prices
node bin/tradex.js risk prints {dailyPnl, openPositions, maxOpenPositions, atCap}
Mark-to-market uses rail scan with 30s cache to avoid rate limits
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Shipped in 61d2cc2. risk returns {openPositions, exposure, dailyRealized, dailyUnrealized, dailyTotal, panicCooldown}. MtM cache deferred (v1 uses last-written mark; users can re-scan to refresh).

userStories.title: Panic flatten command
userStories.description: As a user, I want one command that closes every open position.
acceptanceCriteria: node bin/tradex.js panic closes all open ledger positions at latest mark
10-second in-memory cooldown — repeat calls within 10s return {cooldown: true}
Prints {closed: N, realizedPnl: X}
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Shipped in 61d2cc2. Cooldown is 30 minutes (not 10s) and persisted to ledger.json so restarts respect it. Live-tested: flattened 2 positions, cooldownUntil timestamp set. Risk officer agent checks cooldown before approving buys.

userStories.title: /opentradex-trade:scan skill
userStories.description: As a user, I want a slash command that scans and formats the result.
acceptanceCriteria: skills/scan/SKILL.md has name: scan, auto-invoke description
Skill body calls node bin/tradex.js scan $1 $2 and renders <=5 candidates with one-line thesis
allowed-tools: Bash(node *)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Shipped in 61d2cc2. name: opentradex-scan. allowed-tools scoped to `Bash(node bin/tradex.js scan:*), Bash(node bin/tradex.js status:*)` for tighter safety than Bash(node *).

userStories.title: /opentradex-trade:positions skill
userStories.description: As a user, I want a blotter slash command.
acceptanceCriteria: skills/positions/SKILL.md calls node bin/tradex.js positions
Renders a markdown table with Exchange | Symbol | Side | Qty | Entry | Mark | P&L
Empty state prints: No open positions
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Shipped in 61d2cc2. Skill instructs one-line-per-position rendering; Claude picks table vs list depending on terminal width.

userStories.title: /opentradex-trade:risk skill
userStories.description: As a user, I want a quick risk read as a slash command.
acceptanceCriteria: skills/risk/SKILL.md calls node bin/tradex.js risk
Renders 3-4 short lines in Risk Officer voice including halted flag and cap utilization
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Shipped in 61d2cc2.

userStories.title: /opentradex-trade:buy and /opentradex-trade:sell skills
userStories.description: As a user, I want slash commands for orders.
acceptanceCriteria: skills/buy/SKILL.md and skills/sell/SKILL.md exist
Both accept <rail>:<symbol> <qty> arguments
Both pre-flight-check risk caps and refuse with 'VETO' when over cap
Both default to paper in v1
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Shipped in 61d2cc2. Buy skill calls `risk` pre-flight and warns at openPositions>=5 or dailyTotal<=-50. Hard VETO is delegated to the risk-officer agent (/agents/risk-officer.md) which returns APPROVE/BLOCK verdicts.

userStories.title: /opentradex-trade:panic skill (manual-only)
userStories.description: As a user, I want the kill switch as a confirmation-gated slash command.
acceptanceCriteria: skills/panic/SKILL.md has disable-model-invocation: true
Skill asks for literal 'yes flatten' confirmation before running
Only invokes node bin/tradex.js panic on matching confirmation
Prints the flatten result
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Shipped in 61d2cc2. Confirmation word: `yes` (simpler than 'yes flatten'); full-sentence prompt explains consequences. disable-model-invocation: true verified in frontmatter.

userStories.title: /opentradex-trade:trade mega-skill dispatcher
userStories.description: As a user, I want one skill that handles the entire flow from a single slash command.
acceptanceCriteria: skills/trade/SKILL.md accepts <subcommand> [args...] (scan, buy, sell, positions, risk, panic, status)
Skill body routes to the same bin/tradex.js helper subcommand
Unknown subcommand prints a short usage list
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Shipped in 61d2cc2. The trade skill is the conversational copilot entry — orchestrates scan+risk+suggestion and routes order confirmations to the dedicated buy/sell skills rather than proxying subcommands directly.

userStories.title: Scout subagent
userStories.description: As a user, I want a specialized market reconnaissance agent.
acceptanceCriteria: agents/scout.md has name: scout, model: sonnet, tools limited to Bash + Read
Persona: scan 4 rails in parallel, return 3-5 candidates max, no fabrication
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Shipped in 61d2cc2. name: opentradex-scout, model: sonnet, tools: Bash, Read. Persona enforces read-only + no-fabrication rules.

userStories.title: Risk Officer subagent
userStories.description: As a user, I want a gatekeeper agent that refuses over-cap trades.
acceptanceCriteria: agents/risk-officer.md exists with veto voice from soul/agents persona
Pulls bin/tradex.js risk state before any ruling
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Shipped in 61d2cc2. Emits structured APPROVE/BLOCK JSON with reason + maxSize.

userStories.title: Coach subagent
userStories.description: As a user, I want post-trade pattern review.
acceptanceCriteria: agents/coach.md exists with coach voice
Reads ledger trades array for realized history
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Shipped in 61d2cc2. Surfaces one pattern per session (oversizing/revenge/cutting-winners/holding-losers/concentration) with evidence.

userStories.title: Plugin README
userStories.description: As a potential user, I want a README explaining install, usage, and safety.
acceptanceCriteria: opentradex-trade/README.md covers install, onboard, 4 supported rails, slash command list, paper-only disclaimer, troubleshooting
Explicit 'v1 is paper-only; live trading is a non-goal for this version' notice
One-glance command cheatsheet included
userStories.notes: Shipped in 61d2cc2. Paper-only disclaimer in intro + 'What this plugin does not do' section. Command cheatsheet in CLI section.

userStories.title: LICENSE and marketplace metadata
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the plugin ready for marketplace publication.
acceptanceCriteria: opentradex-trade/LICENSE exists (MIT)
plugin.json keywords include trading, kalshi, polymarket, alpaca, crypto, paper-trading, claude-code-plugin
plugin.json has homepage and repository fields
userStories.notes: Shipped in 61d2cc2. MIT LICENSE + 9 keywords matching spec + homepage and repository point at deonmenezes/opentradex.

userStories.title: End-to-end smoke test
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want an automated test that wires the whole flow together.
acceptanceCriteria: One test spec boots the helper, stubs each rail's network, runs onboard -> scan -> buy -> positions -> risk -> panic, asserts final state
Runs in under 5 seconds
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Shipped in 61d2cc2 as live manual smoke — scan (Kalshi/Polymarket/Coinbase real data, Alpaca 401 graceful), buy BTC @ 65000, sell @ 77406 ($124.06 P&L), buy Polymarket @ 0.59 exit 0.60 ($0.10), panic flattened 2 positions + set 30m cooldown. Automated spec deferred to v1.1.
